Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Inter-American Commission on Human Rights (IACHR) Thematic Hearing on U.S. Counternarcotics Operations in the Caribbean and Eastern Pacific US Dept. of State 13 Mar 2026 -- Today the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights (IACHR) strayed far outside its mandate and acted beyond its competence in holding a thematic hearing on U.S. counter-narcoterrorism operations in the Caribbean and Eastern Pacific.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Hegseth Says Iran's Defense Industrial Base Nearing Complete Destruction DoD News 13 Mar 2026 -- Secretary of War Pete Hegseth told reporters today that, in addition to the extensive damage the U.S. and Israeli militaries have been inflicting upon the Iranian terrorist regime's active combat forces, the country's defense industrial base is now nearing complete destruction as well.
  • Transcript: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Air Force Gen. Dan Caine Hold a Press Briefing DoD 13 Mar 2026
  • All Crew Members of U.S. KC-135 Loss in Iraq Confirmed Deceased CENTCOM 13 Mar 2026 -- All six crew members aboard a U.S. KC-135 refueling aircraft that went down in western Iraq are now confirmed deceased. The aircraft was lost while flying over friendly airspace March 12 during Operation Epic Fury.
  • All 6 American troops killed after resistance downed US aircraft Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- All six crew members of a US Air Force refueling aircraft have been confirmed killed after it was shot down by resistance groups in western Iraq on Thursday.
  • Four Confirmed Deceased in Loss of U.S. KC-135 Over Iraq CENTCOM 13 Mar 2026 -- At approximately 2 pm ET on March 12, a U.S. KC-135 refueling aircraft went down in western Iraq. Four of six crew members on board the aircraft have been confirmed deceased as rescue efforts continue.
  • US burned through 'years' of munitions with war on Iran: Report Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- The administration of US President Donald Trump has burned through "years" of critical munitions since the start of its aggression on Iran, a report says, noting that the country will needs years to replenish its stockpiles.
  • US Warplane Down, French Soldier Killed In Iraq As Iran War Effects Spread RFE/RL 13 Mar 2026 -- A giant US refueling plane crashed in Iraq after an apparent midair collision, while a French soldier was killed in the country's Kurdistan region as the 2-week-old US-Israeli war on Iran continued to spill over regional borders.
  • 2 injured after fire aboard USS Gerald R. Ford in Red Sea: U.S. Navy People's Daily 13 Mar 2026 -- Two sailors aboard USS Gerald R. Ford were injured when a fire broke out in the main laundry spaces of the aircraft carrier operating in the Red Sea, the U.S. Navy said Thursday.

  • IRGC stages 48th wave of Op. True Promise 4 alongside Hezbollah; Zionists warned of 'days of darkness' Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has announced the successful execution of the 48th wave of its ongoing retaliatory Operation True Promise 4 against Zionist and American targets.
  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 13 Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- Iranian armed forces and resistance groups across the region continue to carry out retaliatory military operations against the United States and the Israeli regime.
  • Iran dismisses reports of missile launch towards Turkey Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- Iran's embassy in Ankara has rejected reports that claimed that the Islamic Republic has fired a missile towards Turkey.
  • IRGC: 'Considerable part of Israel's airspace under our control' Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- The commander of Aerospace Force of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says it launched a massive retaliatory operation against Israel, effectively disrupting the regime's aerospace monitoring capabilities.
  • IRGC launches 45th wave of Operation True Promise 4 using solid-fuel missiles Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps announced the launch of the 45th wave of Operation True Promise 4 against American and Israeli targets in response to the ongoing US-Israeli terrorist aggression against Iran.
  • Iran's Army conducts massive drone strike on Israel's military headquarters in Beersheba Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Army has carried out a massive drone strike on the headquarters of the Israeli occupation forces in the Beersheba area, in response to the ongoing US-Israeli terrorist strikes against Iran.
  • IRGC hits Israeli military sites, US bases in major offensive Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has launched the 44th phase of its Operation True Promise 4, targeting a series of key military sites in Israeli occupied lands and US bases across West Asia.
  • IRGC Navy's strike damages US aircraft carrier Abraham Lincoln Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolution Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy has targeted the US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln with missiles and drones, rendering it non-operational and forcing its retreat from regional waters.

  • Türkiye says NATO neutralized ballistic munition fired from Iran into Turkish airspace Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Mar 2026 -- Türkiye said on Friday that a ballistic munition launched from Iran and entering Turkish airspace was "neutralized by NATO air and missile defense assets deployed in the Eastern Mediterranean," Anadolu Agency reported citing the Turkish defense ministry.

  • UK troops at Iraq base shot down Iranian drones, Healey says Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Mar 2026 -- British forces based in Iraq shot down two Iranian drones overnight, Defence Secretary John Healey has said, BBC reported.

  • French soldier killed during attack in Iraq's Erbil region: Macron Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Mar 2026 -- French president announced early Friday that a French soldier was killed and several others injured during an attack in Iraq's Erbil region, Anadolu Agency reported.

  • Iraqi resistance targets two US aircraft, launches 37 operations on bases Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Resistance in Iraq has claimed responsibility for downing a US KC-135 refueling aircraft and conducting 37 attacks on US bases in the region over the past 24 hours.

  • Trump Claims US Has 'Virtually Unlimited' Ammunition, Operations Could Continue 'Forever' Sputnik 13 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump claimed on Friday the Pentagon has "virtually unlimited" stocks of ammunition allowing the military to continue operations against Iran for as long as required.
  • United States to Launch Very Powerful Strikes on Iran Over Next Week - Trump Sputnik 13 Mar 2026 -- The United States plans to launch very powerful strikes against Iran over the next week, US President Donald Trump said.
  • US Navy May Escort Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Part of International Coalition - Bessent Sputnik 13 Mar 2026 -- US Secretary of the Treasury Scott Bessent said on Thursday that the US Navy may escort vessels through the Strait of Hormuz as part of an international coalition.
  • White House divided as Trump struggles to find exit strategy from Iran war: Report Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- A deepening internal tug-of-war is reportedly paralyzing the White House, as US President Donald Trump's advisors clash over how to define "victory" amid a global energy crisis and significant strategic miscalculations regarding the unassailable resilience of the Iranian nation.
  • Trump Says Russia Might Be Helping Iran 'A Little Bit' In The War RFE/RL 13 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said it is possible that Russian President Vladimir Putin is helping Iran in its war with the United States and Israel.
  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Senators Warren, Schumer Statement on Trump Administration's Further Weakening of Russia Sanctions as Trump's Iran War Drives Up Costs US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 13 Mar 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Elizabeth Warren (D-MA), Ranking Member of the Senate Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs Committee, and Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-NY) released the following statement:

  • Top Iranian Officials Take To The Streets In Tehran As Hegseth Warns Of Intense US-Israeli Air Attacks RFE/RL 13 Mar 2026 -- Several senior Iranian officials, including President Masud Pezeshkian, took to the streets of Tehran for the annual Al-Quds Day rally in support of the Palestinian cause despite an intense wave of air strikes by the United States and Israel.
  • Iran views US, Israel as equally criminal; war to continue until enemies regret aggression: Parl. speaker Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Majlis (Parliament) speaker says, in the eyes of the Islamic Republic, the United States and the Israeli regime are no longer different from one another as far as the gravity of their crimes versus the Iranian nation is concerned.
  • Pezeshkian tells Modi: Iran not seeking war, fighting 'savage' US-Israeli aggression Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian says Iran does not seek war but is exercising its right to self-defense in the face of "savage" US-Israeli aggression.
  • Iran defends right to self-defense in Strait of Hormuz, slams US aggression Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- Iran has emphasized its commitment to the principle of freedom of navigation but stated that the ongoing tensions in the region, particularly in the Strait of Hormuz, are a direct result of as the destabilizing actions of the United States.
  • Iran's new supreme leader urges continued closure of Hormuz Strait, vows to open new battlefronts People's Daily 13 Mar 2026 -- Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei on Thursday called for the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz and pledged to open new fronts in his country's conflict with the United States and Israel.

  • Statement by Tom Fletcher, USG for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator: Closure of Hormuz could have 'immense impact' on humanitarian operations UNOCHA 13 Mar 2026

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in the Empty Quarter SPA 13 Mar 2026

  • Foreign Minister, Canadian Counterpart Discuss Regional Escalation SPA 13 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah received a phone call today from Canadian Minister of Foreign Affairs Anita Anand, during which they discussed the latest developments in the regional escalation and efforts to maintain security and stability
  • Saudi, Korean Foreign Ministers Review Regional Developments SPA 13 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah received a phone call today from Korean Minister of Foreign Affairs Cho Hyun, during which they discussed the latest developments in the regional escalation, as well as topics of common interest
  • HRH the Crown Prince, French President Discuss Regional Military Escalation in Phone Call SPA 13 Mar 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, received a phone call today from President Emmanuel Macron of the French Republic, during which they discussed the military escalation in the region and its repercussions on security and stability

  • HE GCCSG: Joint Ministerial Meetings with Jordan, Egypt, Morocco, and the UK Achieved Positive Results in Strengthening Relations; We Laud Their Firm and Supportive Stance in Standing with the GCC Against Brutal Iranian Aggressions Gulf Cooperation Council 13 Mar 2026
  • ASEAN Chair's Statement on the Outcomes of the Special Asean Foreign Ministers' Meeting on the Situation in the Middle East Philippines DFA 13 Mar 2026
  • Philippines Convenes Special ASEAN Foreign Ministers' Meeting on the Middle East Security Crisis Philippines DFA 13 Mar 2026 -- The Philippines, in its capacity as ASEAN Chair, convened a Special ASEAN Foreign Ministers' Meeting on 13 March 2026 to address the escalating security crisis in the Middle East and its far-reaching implications for regional economic stability, energy security, and the safety of ASEAN nationals in affected areas.
  • Media Statement of Secretary of Foreign Affairs Ma. Theresa Lazaro Special ASEAN Foreign Ministers Meeting on the Situation in the Middle East Philippines DFA 13 Mar 2026
  • Spanish Government strongly condemns attacks in Iraq against France and Italy and expresses condolences for death of French soldier Spain MFA 13 Mar 2026
  • "Keeping our country away from this fire hole is our top priority" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 13 Mar 2026 -- Speaking at the 10th National Will Iftar, President Erdoğan said: "Just like last night, we are carrying out necessary preventive activities against every kind of threat that violates our airspace. Türkiye's thousands-year-old ancient statecraft and our government's 23-year-old crisis management experience are our biggest guides in analyzing the complicated incidents in our vicinity and giving them appropriate responses. Keeping our country away from this fire hole is our top priority."

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Joint Interagency Task Force Spearheads Contract, Unifies Drone Defenses DoD News 13 Mar 2026 -- To counter the rapidly evolving threat from hostile unmanned aircraft systems, Joint Interagency Task Force 401 has championed a groundbreaking enterprise-level agreement to provide a cutting-edge command-and-control solution through a strategic action.
  • U.S. Army awards enterprise contract for IT commercial solutions Army News 13 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Army awarded Anduril a new enterprise contract to consolidate the procurement and management of the company's commercially available technologies. This strategic move will streamline operations, reduce administrative costs, and accelerate the fielding of critical capabilities to Warfighters and other stakeholders across the U.S. Government.
  • Army forges partnership with Pennsylvania companies to create next generation small arms gun barrel Army News 13 Mar 2026 -- Army armament engineers here partnered with a pair of Pennsylvania companies, one with alloy expertise and the other with forging metals, to deliver a capable barrel for the Army's next generation of small arms.

  • Press conference by Federal Chancellor Merz, Norway's Prime Minister Støre and Canada's Prime Minister Carney - NATO's Cold Response exercise: defending security on NATO's northern flank Germany - Federal Government 13 Mar 2026

  • Contacts With Russian Defense Ministry Very Important for IAEA - Grossi Sputnik 13 Mar 2026 -- Contacts with the Russian Defense Ministry are very important for the International Atomic Energy Agency given that the Zaporozhye Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP) is located in a war zone, IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi said on Friday.

  • Intercepting Kinzhal, Iskander, and Zircon missiles: key facts about PAC-3 missiles for the Patriot air defense system delivered to Ukraine by Germany Ukraine MOD 13 Mar 2026
  • Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, and the President of Ukraine, Volodymyr Zelensky, at the 86th Air Base, in Borcea Romania - Ministry of National Defence 13 Mar 2026 -- On Thursday, March 12, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, and the President of Ukraine, Volodymyr Zelensky, paid a visit to the 86th Air Base "Lieutenant Aviator Gheorghe Mociorniță" in Borcea, Calarasi County.

  • PLA Navy's new Type 052D destroyer conducts maiden training with newly debuted Type 055 destroyer, official media reports show Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- Yet another Type 052D guided missile destroyer of the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Navy, recently conducted its maiden training - together with a newly debuted Type 055 large destroyer, according to official media reports on Thursday.

  • Legislative Yuan Authorizes Signing of Four Military Procurement Letters of Offer and Acceptance; Ministry of National Defense Clarifies Procedures for the Special Defense Act ROC MND 13 Mar 2026
  • Legislature authorizes Cabinet to sign letters securing U.S. weapons CNA 13 Mar 2026 -- Taiwan's Legislative Yuan on Friday passed a motion authorizing the Cabinet to sign letters of acceptance (LOA) for four weapons systems approved for sale by the United States that were set to expire this month.

  • North Korea Condemns Japan's Long-Range Missile Deployment - Reports Sputnik 13 Mar 2026 -- North Korea condemns Japan's plans to deploy long-range missiles, warning such moves heighten regional tensions and threaten security, North Korean state-run news agency KCNA reported on Friday.

  • Leap forward for Henderson Defence Precinct Australia DOD 13 Mar 2026 -- The delivery of the Henderson Defence Precinct has taken a significant step forward with the design services consultancy contract awarded to Bechtel Infrastructure Australia Pty Ltd (Bechtel).
  • Amphibious force storms Queensland beach Australia DOD 13 Mar 2026 -- More than 1000 soldiers, sailors and aviators aboard HMA Ships Canberra and Choules participated in Exercise Wader last month, to validate the ADF's ability to project and sustain combat power from sea to shore.

  • S. Korea and Canada Sign Agreement on the Protection of Military and Defense Classified Information ROK MND 13 Mar 2026 -- South Korea and Canada have signed the "Agreement on the Protection of Military and Defense Classified Information." The two governments have also initiated measures to commence negotiations on a Defense Cooperation Agreement.

  • EDA signs contract for military satellite research project European Defence Agency 13 Mar 2026 -- The European Defence Agency (EDA) signed a €15.65 million research contract on 13 March 2026 with an industry consortium to explore the Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) region in space.

  • Prime Ministers see first-hand how Allies are training to defend Norway Government of Norway 13 Mar 2026 -- Today, Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney visited NATO forces taking part in the Cold Response military exercise.

  • Speech by the Minister for Foreign Affairs at the Baltic Sea Security Talks in Gdansk Government Offices of Sweden 13 Mar 2026

  • UK and Ireland agree updated Memorandum of Understanding on bilateral defence cooperation UK MOD 13 Mar 2026 -- The UK and Ireland will be better protected against common threats as the two nations sign a refreshed Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to enhance bilateral defence cooperation.

  • Suren Papikyan met with the Chairman of the EU Military Committee Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 13 Mar 2026
  • Suren Papikyan met with the EU Commissioner for Defence and Space Andrius Kubilius in Brussels Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 13 Mar 2026

  • Prime Minister: Azerbaijan exports military products to almost 20 countries Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Mar 2026 -- "The state budget for 2025 also increased expenditures on defense and national security. This demonstrates that ensuring the sovereignty, territorial integrity, and national security of our country is one of the key priorities of state policy," said Ali Asadov, Prime Minister of Azerbaijan, as he addressed the Milli Majlis of the Republic of Azerbaijan on March 13.

  • US assets to stay in PH, no redeployment to Mideast Philippine News Agency 13 Mar 2026 -- There are no indications that the United States is withdrawing its military assets from the Philippines despite the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, a senior military official said Friday.
  • BRP Diego Silang, PH Navy contingent now in Australia for 'Kakadu' Philippine News Agency 13 Mar 2026 -- The Philippine Navy's Naval Defense Command (NDC) on Friday said its guided missile frigate, BRP Diego Silang (FFG-7), and the naval contingent aboard it have safely arrived in Darwin, Australia for the "International Fleet Review" (IFR) and this year's iteration of Multilateral Naval Exercise "Kakadu."
  • Resilient military infra vital to PH defense - Brawner Philippine News Agency 13 Mar 2026 -- Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) chief Gen. Romeo Brawner Jr. reiterated the value of a resilient military infrastructure in a nation's defense particularly an archipelagic one.

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 13 March 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 13 Mar 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Israel's Second War: The Fight Against Iran's Proxy, Hezbollah RFE/RL 13 Mar 2026 -- As the US-Israeli war with Iran continues, a second front against Hezbollah has led to the displacement of some 800,000 people fleeing deadly Israeli air strikes in Lebanon, while a succession of rocket and drone attacks has rained down on Israel.
  • Hezbollah: Netanyahu should be fearful; resistance our only option Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- Hezbollah's secretary-general issues a strong warning to Benjamin Netanyahu after the Israeli prime minister threatened to assassinate him.
  • Dozens of Israeli forces injured as Hezbollah operations shake northern occupied lands Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- A series of Hezbollah rocket operations targeting Israeli troop concentrations along the border triggered widespread sirens across northern Israel early Friday, with Israeli media reporting at least 50 people were injured, as an Iranian missile also struck the community of Zarzir, wounding some 60 settlers.
  • Israel targets building in central Beirut in new wave of strikes People's Daily 13 Mar 2026 -- The Israeli military announced on Thursday afternoon that it launched a new series of airstrikes in Beirut targeting Hezbollah targets.

  • UN chief launches major humanitarian appeal from war-torn Lebanon UN News 13 Mar 2026 -- In a solidarity visit to the war-ravaged capital of Lebanon, the UN chief on Friday announced a flash humanitarian appeal of $308.3 million to support civilians there, after ongoing Israeli strikes countering rocket launches from Hezbollah left around 816,000 Lebanese internally displaced, a figure that looks set to rise.
  • Middle East war: Women in Lebanon forced to give birth on roadside UN News 13 Mar 2026 -- As the UN Secretary-General touched down in Beirut on Friday in solidarity with the people of Lebanon, UN agencies highlighted the dangers for civilians and particularly pregnant women and migrant workers, amid ongoing airstrikes and rocket fire between Hezbollah fighters and Israel.
  • Secretary-General's Press Stakeout After Meeting with Speaker of the Lebanese House of Parliament Nabih Berri UN Secretary-General 13 Mar 2026
  • Secretary-General's remarks at the Launch of the Flash Humanitarian Appeal for Lebanon UN Secretary-General 13 Mar 2026
  • Secretary-General's remarks after meeting with the President of the Republic of Lebanon Joseph Aoun UN Secretary-General 13 Mar 2026
  • Amid protection crisis in Lebanon, UN experts warn bombing civilians to force displacement is unlawful OHCHR 13 Mar 2026 -- UN experts* today raised the alarm over a rapidly escalating displacement crisis in Lebanon, after intense Israeli airstrikes and evacuation warnings in southern Lebanon and parts of Beirut.
  • Nearly One Million Displaced in Lebanon, IOM Appeals for USD 19 Million IOM 13 Mar 2026 -- The International Organization for Migration (IOM) is appealing for USD 19 million to scale up its emergency response in Lebanon, where the number of people forced to flee their homes has surged to nearly one million people following a sharp escalation of hostilities since 2 March.

  • Norway increases support for victims of the war in the Middle East Government of Norway 13 Mar 2026 -- Norway is allocating NOK 25 million (approximately USD 2.5 million) from the humanitarian reserve to alleviate the humanitarian situation in the region. The funds will be disbursed to the Red Cross and the UN.
  • General Secretariats of the OIC, the League of Arab States, and the African Union Commission Condemn the Israeli occupation authorities' continued closure of the blessed Al-Aqsa Mosque Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 13 Mar 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC), the General Secretariat of the League of Arab States (LAS), and the African Union Commission (AUC) affirm the central status of the Holy City of Al-Quds (Jerusalem), as well as the established religious rights and the eternal bond of Muslims with the blessed Al-Aqsa Mosque, the first of the two qiblas and the third of the two holy sanctuaries
  • Iceland, Netherlands join South Africa's ICJ case against Israeli genocide in Gaza Press TV 13 Mar 2026 -- Iceland and the Netherlands have filed declarations to intervene in South Africa's genocide case against Israel at the International Court of Justice (ICJ), the United Nations' highest court.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Middle East, Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory UNOCHA 13 Mar 2026

  • Pakistan-Afghanistan Border Strikes Undermine China's Diplomatic Push For Peace RFE/RL 13 Mar 2026 -- Fresh Pakistani jet and drone strikes targeting Kabul and the Kandahar, Paktika, and Paktia provinces in Afghanistan have cast a shadow over China's shuttle-diplomacy efforts to restore peace between Pakistan and the Taliban.
  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with Afghan Foreign Minister Mawlawi Amir Khan Muttaqi PRC MofA 13 Mar 2026 -- On March 13, 2026, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi had a phone call with Afghan Foreign Minister Mawlawi Amir Khan Muttaqi at the latter's request.

  • HRC61 - EU Statement - Interactive Dialogue with Commission of inquiry on the Syrian Arab Republic European External Action Service (EEAS) 13 Mar 2026
  • UN Syria Commission calls for justice, reform, and international support as Syria's fragile transition remains undermined by ongoing violations OHCHR 13 Mar 2026 -- The Independent International Commission of Inquiry on the Syrian Arab Republic today released its latest report, outlining both the progress and the profound challenges facing Syria as it continues its transition toward inclusive governance, rule of law, and accountability.
  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Statement for the Interactive Dialogue on Syria UK FCDO 13 Mar 2026 -- UK Statement for the Interactive Dialogue with the Commission of Inquiry on the Syrian Arab Republic. Delivered by the UK's Permanent Representative to the WTO and UN, Kumar lyer.

  • HRC61 - EU Statement - Interactive Dialogue with the Special Rapporteur on the situation of human rights in Myanmar European External Action Service (EEAS) 13 Mar 2026
  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Statement for the Special Rapporteur on Myanmar UK FCDO 13 Mar 2026 -- UK Statement for the Interactive Dialogue with the Special Rapporteur on Myanmar. Delivered by the UK's Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.
  • Myanmar at a 'crossroads': The world must not forsake civilians there, urges UN expert UN News 13 Mar 2026 -- More than five years after Myanmar's military coup, international resolve to hold the junta accountable must not weaken, an independent human rights expert warned on Friday, as escalating violence and growing humanitarian needs push millions of civilians deeper into crisis.

News Reports

  • Intensive work with the investigation on Sea Owl I Swedish Coast Guard 13 Mar 2026 -- The ship Sea Owl I, which was boarded by the Swedish Coast Guard and the Swedish Police on Thursday evening, is anchored south of Trelleborg, Sweden. Caffa is also anchored in the same area. The Swedish Coast Guard is working intensively with the ongoing investigations, led by the Swedish Prosecution Authority.

  • Japan approves bill to set up intelligence body; move stirs up tensions in Asia-Pacific and fuels resurgence of Japanese militarism: Chinese expert Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- The Japanese government on Friday signed off on a bill to create a "National Intelligence Council," a new command center for intelligence policy headed by the prime minister, and an operational "National Intelligence Agency," Asahi Shimbun reported.

  • US Authorizes Sale of Russian Crude Oil Loaded On Vessels as of March 12 - OFAC Sputnik 13 Mar 2026 -- The United States has authorized the sale of Russian crude oil and petroleum products loaded onto vessels as of March 12, a general license from the US Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) read.
  • Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council President of Russia 13 Mar 2026 -- The President held a briefing session with permanent members of the Security Council via videoconference.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's opening remarks during his meeting with Executive Secretary of the CTBTO Preparatory Commission Robert Floyd, Moscow, March 13, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Mar 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Executive Secretary of the CTBTO Preparatory Commission Robert Floyd Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's opening remarks at a meeting with IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi, Moscow, March 13, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Mar 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna: increasingly decisive steps are being taken to deter the shadow fleet Estonia MFA 13 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna said today, 13 March, that despite developments in the Middle East, the attention of European countries has not shifted away from Russia's aggression against Ukraine and the need to deter Russia's shadow fleet.

  • China opposes undermining intl economic, trade order through 'small-circle' rules, FM on US-Japan-EU critical minerals plan Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- Maintaining an open, inclusive and mutually beneficial international trade environment serves the common interests of all countries, and all parties have a responsibility to play a constructive role in safeguarding the stability and security of global critical minerals supply chains, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday.
  • US' so-called 'forced labor' claims entirely fabricated, China opposes using it as pretext for political manipulation: FM on Section 301 probe Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- China on Friday slammed the US for concocting a lie with so-called "forced labor" claims and expressed opposition to using it as a pretext for political manipulation.
  • Chinese FM responds to media inquiry on China's expectations on upcoming 6th round of China-US economic and trade consultations Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- In response to a media inquiry on the specific agenda and the Chinese side's expectations on an economic and trade talk in Paris this weekend between Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng, also a member of the Political Bureau of the Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee, and US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday that the Chinese side has already released information concerning the sixth round of China-US trade consultations.
  • US Section 301 probe a typical act of unilateralism that seriously undermines international economic and trade order: MOFCOM Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- Asked by a media reporter for comment on the Office of the US Trade Representative's announcement on Wednesday US Eastern Time of the launch of Section 301 investigations against 16 economies including China, citing so-called "excess capacity," a spokesperson for China's Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) said on Friday that China has noted the situation. The Section 301 investigation is a typical act of unilateralism that seriously undermines the international economic and trade order, and a World Trade Organization (WTO) panel has already ruled that the tariff measures taken based on Section 301 investigations violate WTO rules, the spokesperson said.
  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with Dutch Foreign Minister Tom Berendsen PRC MofA 13 Mar 2026 -- On March 13, 2026, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi had a phone call with Dutch Foreign Minister Tom Berendsen at the latter's request.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on March 13, 2026 PRC MofA 13 Mar 2026
  • China's national legislature concludes annual session; 'effort to project stability, policy continuity carries international significance' Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- The 14th National People's Congress (NPC), China's national legislature, held the closing meeting of its fourth session on Thursday.
  • China's national legislature concludes annual session People's Daily 13 Mar 2026 -- The 14th National People's Congress (NPC), China's national legislature, concluded its fourth session Thursday.
  • China approves 2026-2030 blueprint, maps out high-quality path toward modernization People's Daily 13 Mar 2026 -- China's national lawmakers on Thursday approved a development blueprint for the 2026-2030 period, marking a pivotal step in the modernization drive of nearly one-fifth of the world's population.
  • HKSAR gov't actively aligns with national 15th Five-Year Plan to promote high-quality development: chief executive People's Daily 13 Mar 2026 -- John Lee, chief executive of China's Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR), said on Thursday that Hong Kong will actively align with the outline of the 15th Five-Year Plan to contribute to further deepening reform and advancing Chinese modernization.
  • China to conduct in-orbit upgrade of BeiDou Navigation Satellite System Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- China will carry out an in-orbit upgrade of the BeiDou Navigation Satellite System (BDS) in the near future to further improve service quality and optimize the operational status of some satellites, the Global Times learned from the China Satellite Navigation Office on Friday.
  • China launches two new test satellites Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- China on Friday sent two new test satellites into space from the Xichang Satellite Launch Center in the southwestern province of Sichuan.
  • China's Long March-8A rocket launches new internet satellites Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- China launched a Long March-8A carrier rocket on Friday in the southern island province of Hainan, sending a new group of internet satellites into space.

  • China's opposition to US arms sales to Taiwan region is consistent, FM says after report claims new package could be approved following Trump's China visit Global Times 13 Mar 2026 -- China's opposition to US arms sales to Taiwan region is consistent and clear. The US should strictly abide by the one-China principle and the three China-US joint communiqués, particularly the August 17 communiqué, stop arms sales to Taiwan, and take concrete actions to safeguard the stable development of China-US relations as well as peace and stability across the Taiwan Straits, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday in response to a media inquiry on a Reuters report that claimed a major US arms package for Taiwan that includes advanced interceptor missiles is ready for US President Donald Trump's approval and could be signed after his trip to China.
  • Ministry of National Defense Issues Press Release Regarding "PRC Launch of a Carrier Rocket Carrying Satellites" ROC MND 13 Mar 2026
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 13 Mar 2026

  • HRC61 - EU Statement - Interactive Dialogue with the Special Rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea European External Action Service (EEAS) 13 Mar 2026
  • Monitoring and surveillance activities by Australia against illicit maritime activities including ship-to-ship transfers Ministry of Defense of Japan 13 Mar 2026
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Devotes Efforts to Forest Restoration KCNA 13 Mar 2026 -- March 14 is the tree-planting day in the DPRK.
  • Regional Security Environment Is Getting Severe Due to Japanese Militarism: KCNA Commentary KCNA 13 Mar 2026 -- Japan's moves for reviving militarism, aimed at rearmament, are leading to full-scale deployment of preemptive attack means for action.

  • S. Korea, U.S. to fast-track deals on nuclear subs, energy, and shipbuilding Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 13 Mar 2026 -- South Korean Prime Minister Kim Min-seok called on U.S. Vice President JD Vance to work together to swiftly implement bilateral agreements on South Korea's push to build nuclear-powered submarines, as well as nuclear energy and shipbuilding, during talks in Washington on Thursday, his office said, Yonhap reported.
  • Nat'l Assembly passes special bill on USD 350B investment in US KOREA.net 13 Mar 2026 -- The National Assembly on March 12 in a plenary session passed a bill on the Special Act on the Operation and Management of Strategic Investments between Korea and the U.S.

  • Investigation Highlights Iran's Growing Footprint In Georgia RFE/RL 13 Mar 2026 -- Georgia's State Security Service has launched an investigation into claims that Iran is expanding its influence in the country through Georgian Shi'ite communities, growing concerns about Tehran's religious and political outreach in the South Caucasus country.

  • Statement between Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Taoiseach Micheál Martin in Cork: 13 March 2026 Gov.UK 13 Mar 2026

  • HRC61 - EU Statement - Interactive Dialogue with the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission on the Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela European External Action Service (EEAS) 13 Mar 2026

  • Gitanas Nausėda and the Czech leader visited Rukla President of the Republic of Lithuania 13 Mar 2026 -- On Friday, Gitanas Nausėda visited the Rukla military hub together with Petr Pavel, President of the Czech Republic, who is on a state visit to Lithuania.

  • Remarks by the First Secretary of the Communist Party of Cuba and President of the Republic, Miguel Díaz-Canel Bermúdez, on the current state of relations with the Government of the United States, during a meeting with members of the Political Bureau, the Secretariat of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of Cuba, and the Executive Committee of the Council of Ministers Cuba Ministry of Foreign Affairs 13 Mar 2026

  • World News in Brief: Attacks on synagogues, Syria's continuing rights violations, shocking abuse of women during childbirth UN News 13 Mar 2026 -- The first two weeks of March have seen a surge in violent attacks and acts of intimidation targeting synagogues and Jewish institutions across North America and Europe.
